title: Action Intent ‚Äî is `destructive` a prominence tier, or one member of a semantic-tone axis separate from `level`?
summary: Prep research for decision #1337 (carved out of #1324, the disposition axis). Action Intent (we:src/_data/intents/action.json:10-18) declares `level: primary | secondary | tertiary | destructive` as 'the semantic weight determining visual prominence' ‚Äî but `destructive` (line 16) is not a prominence value. primary/secondary/tertiary form an ORDINAL scale (how loud); `destructive` is a NOMINAL semantic (what kind of consequence ‚Äî danger/harm/irreversibility). Seating it in the same single-select enum makes prominence and danger MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE, so 'primary destructive' (a loud red Delete) and 'tertiary destructive' (a quiet red Delete link) are both INEXPRESSIBLE ‚Äî the exact orthogonality break #1318 found for `variant` and #1324 found for `disposition`, one axis over. FINDING 1 ‚Äî no design system models danger as a prominence tier; every benchmark gives it a SEPARATE axis, and that axis carries SIBLINGS (success/warning/info), not just danger: Bootstrap contextual classes (primary/secondary/success/danger/warning/info/light/dark), Material 3 color roles + `error` role, Blueprint `intent={none|primary|success|warning|danger}`, Chakra `colorScheme`/`status`, Ant `status`/`danger`, Fluent `appearance`+severity, Radix `color`. The shared shape is a NOMINAL semantic-tone axis orthogonal to emphasis/prominence. FINDING 2 ‚Äî SwiftUI is the apparent counter-example (`ButtonRole={cancel, destructive}` folds danger into a 'role') but that role drives confirmation-styling + ordering, NOT prominence, and `destructive` there is ALSO orthogonal to `cancel` (disposition) ‚Äî confirming danger is its own axis, not a member of prominence OR disposition. A destructive action is usually the AFFIRMATIVE one (Delete = confirm the deletion), so danger ‚âÝ disposition either; it is a third orthogonal axis. FINDING 3 ‚Äî the web platform has NO native vocabulary for semantic tone (no `<button tone>`/`severity`; ARIA has no danger state; `aria-invalid` is validation, not action-tone) ‚Äî like emphasis (#1318) it is a pure design-system convention; borrow the convergent DS vocabulary, standardize the meta-axis not a closed list. FINDING 4 ‚Äî WE precedent is unanimous: #1318 added `variant` and #1324 added `disposition` as new orthogonal dimensions on Action Intent rather than overloading `level`; this is the third application of the same move. CLASSIFICATION: intent layer, dimension (not protocol/trait), expose whole axis, all values legitimate simultaneous end-states (a footer can hold neutral + danger actions), explicit per-action (NOT DI/ambient), most-permissive default = unspecified/neutral, open-numbered per #1318 contract, seam = intent‚Üínative/theme color wiring (danger ‚Üí conventional red treatment + optional confirmation affordance, which stays native). RULING SHAPE: Fork 1 RELOCATE `destructive` off `level` (keep-on-level is broken ‚Äî forces danger‚ä•prominence mutually exclusive); Fork 2 model a FULL open-numbered semantic-tone axis (`neutral|danger` core + `success|warning|info` recommended extended) vs a narrow `danger`-only flag (under-fits, forces a second relocation later); Fork 3 NAME the axis (`tone` recommended; `status`/`severity`/`intent` alternatives ‚Äî `intent` collides with WE's Intent concept). SETTLED: primary/secondary/tertiary stay a clean ordinal `level`; behavioral confirmation stays native; migration (deprecated `level=destructive` alias ‚Üí `tone=danger`) is a separately-prioritized build, not a merit fork. Full grounding in we:reports/2026-06-20-action-tone-semantic-axis.md.
